summary: No ship: the locked RED tests correctly exercise the real call sites, but the plan still permits the obsolete six-case gate. Simpler alternative: make the embedded nine-case script the sole normative gate definition and cross-reference it everywhere else. Open question: none.

findings.title: Normative checklist still specifies the obsolete six-case selftest
findings.body: The B-1 increment still requires a “6-fixture” selftest, and the release checklist repeats that count at line 2284, while the revised gate requires nine cases: (g) missing witness, (h) early match against a large listing, and (i) listing failure are what pin the accepted P-38/P-39 discovery fixes. An engineer following the increment/checklist can omit those three cases and still claim plan compliance, leaving listing-status and SIGPIPE regressions untested.
findings.recommendation: Update every normative six-case reference to nine cases, explicitly require (a)–(i), and state that the six parser predicates plus M-SIGPIPE and M-OLDPARSER must all be mutation-killed.

next_steps: Correct the stale B-1 and B-5 selftest requirements.
Re-run the plan review after the document consistently requires the nine-case gate.
